title: Bastardia bivalvis
taxonomy: Bastardia bivalvis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It belongs to the order Apiales and the class Magnoliopsida. This classification places it within the larger group of flowering plants. The plant's family, Apiaceae, is commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. Within the Apiaceae family, Bastardia bivalvis is a distinct species.

side_effects: Bastardia bivalvis is a plant that may have potential side effects due to its toxicity. Its seeds and leaves contain toxic compounds that can cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal problems if ingested. Ingesting large quantities of Bastardia bivalvis can lead to more severe health issues, including vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain.
